THE BASIS FOR WITHDRAWAL MOTION FILED ON JANUARY 26, 2021 IS LACK OF COMMUNICATION WITH CLIENT. BASED ON THE SUBMISSION OF CLIENT DECLARATION DATED FEBRUARY 3, 2021 IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO VACATE ORDER DEEMING ADMISSIONS ADMITTED, THE LINE OF COMMUNICATION HAS BEEN REESTABLISHED. MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kelly Van Aken, Esq. 415-539-3100 for Plaintiff.
Richard Beckman, Esq. 415-871-0070 of Beckman Feller Chang P.C. for Defendant.
Jonathan Tijerino, son of Octavio Tejerino is also present on behalf of his father.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT OCTAVIO TIJERINO, MARITZA TIJERINO's Motion To Set Aside Admissions Order.

Granted on the contingency of Defendant appearing for his Deposition per written order. Plaintiff to send a proposed order in word format approved as to form directly to the Judge Pro Tem at tim@ncalegal.com.
